---
layout: default
title: "National Political Brief: Legislative Reforms and Parliamentary Developments"
date: 2026-07-11T15:28:17.612935+00:00
summary: "Parliament advances key legislative agendas while political parties clash over election reforms, donation transparency, and allegations of horse-trading"
run_time_ist: "8:58PM"
---

<article class="digest-post">
  <a class="back-link" href="{{ '/' | relative_url }}">Political Brief</a>
  <p class="post-meta">Gemini Summary: 8:58PM</p>


<h2 class="section-title">India National Politics</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Ram Temple donations:</strong> The Congress party has formally requested a Supreme Court-monitored probe into alleged financial irregularities and theft concerning donations made to the Ram Temple.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I1</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I2</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I3</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiogFBVV95cUxPcGtMX3R3Y3FqUnc4a0tvamJTS0N1RGhhNkRlVENBcnNzVGppZUZ1MWMwY3NEX2psbVAwYWEzUW54OC1mUGphM1d4NkJseGJtWjd1N1NqSEVQbkM0OXZMQ2RMdHh6MkpBOFdscHQ0OURUelpCbnltUUNzZG84Y2U2T3E1amVrZ0VOdHIwN0labHAtSTZSOVJZaFRJRmRncEFVSFE?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I11</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Parliamentary legislative activity:</strong> Home Minister Amit Shah addressed the Lok Sabha regarding the Delimitation Bill, the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, and the Union Territories Laws (Amendment) Bill, while the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, was passed.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMixAFBVV95cUxQU3ZPV0tBQ0k0Q2ZRTFZWZ1JVTjIyOWY4U2VVcEtUMzNURDBlSUdheU1TamkwWWZSQTczRXR2ZUttTUZkdnBJS2JhdFRQTmlIamtZZEpEQ2ROOVJISm9OSXhEYk84TmUyZUJOTHgzci1PaVFSeTlHMWpzQV9rOWEwWHNualRibzRieVJzSXNRY1ZDdVVsSGhGdEVFRjZXeEhoSnFkS2RnbUNpRFFfaFFiWEQwQUVSbGZiZW91ZFpnTjZOLVF0?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I7</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMifEFVX3lxTE5NUmxlOFYxbkc2bWdTWkJmUjZYb0otV2c5VGVld2poazVEVGlRdWJjUjFmT181cFg5Nnc1VXRnZ3BWOVNjQzd2TWkxYndybkNxdnVjZGs0UmtsODlDVmpEYVMwZmVhZDJRanprU0R0bDllbW0yUm1hZ1lyXzk?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I9</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMifEFVX3lxTFBWX0t6Nlh6eVZ4ci14RkxreGdvQ2lMQUt4VDh0bmNNMDI1V1I0bnloYXZ3dU5CSzVsSW1hN2FVV0cycGZ2ZUY4aXVDTmtWaW5rRWFZYl9UNXMtR2lWdGRHc0hSQV8wRTRKUFVpeVdtTTlZd3dFMk9leklBbnM?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I10</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Rajya Sabha elections:</strong> BJP candidate Sanjay Bhatia and Congress candidate Karamvir Singh Boudh have secured Rajya Sabha seats in the latest Haryana state elections.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqwFBVV95cUxNT3V5YWExT3MtWkhqcmVod3l0V3lGNWk0Y0J2ODNHdDJBbFFEQ1BXTEFsRTB4ZDJhbGZVWnp6cWRlSWgxekN2Njh5RkItYXh0azNnUWdQb0dOOE9TWGJ4a0dkOUViSWVJTGh0akU5X2QtWEZrc3NaV1VENlQzVGlzQ2NFNVc4VjNXVDdhM1BPdjhEWFQyRmppZF9uQzBibTE3bWE4cWhlem5kRGM?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I12</a></span></li>
</ul>

<h2 class="section-title">Policy and Institutions</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>One Nation, One Election:</strong> A parliamentary panel, citing former chief justices, asserted that the proposed simultaneous elections would not violate the Constitution, receiving support from regional leaders.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W3</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Allegations of political poaching:</strong> Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah has alleged that the BJP attempted to induce National Conference MLAs to switch sides through financial offers, a charge that highlights ongoing tensions in regional governance.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W1</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W2</a></span></li>
</ul>
